install.title: Install Blok
install.description: Add Blok to your project using your favorite package manager.

configure.title: Import and configure
configure.description: Import the editor and tools, then configure your block types.

save.title: Save content
save.description: Extract clean JSON data ready to save anywhere.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.description: Get up and running with Blok in just a few simple steps.

blokClass.title: Blok Class
blokClass.description: The main editor class that initializes and manages the Blok editor instance.

save.name: save()
save.description: Extracts the current editor content as structured JSON data. This is the primary method for persisting editor content.

render.name: render(data)
render.description: Renders editor content from previously saved JSON data.

focus.name: focus(atEnd?)
focus.description: Sets focus to the editor. Optionally positions cursor at the end of content.

clear.name: clear()
clear.description: Removes all blocks from the editor.

destroy.name: destroy()
destroy.description: Destroys the editor instance and removes all DOM elements and event listeners.

isReady.name: isReady
isReady.description: Promise that resolves when editor is ready

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.description: The configuration object passed to the Blok constructor.

blocksApi.title: Blocks API
blocksApi.description: Manage blocks in the editor — create, delete, update, and reorder content.

blockApi.title: BlockAPI
blockApi.description: Interface for working with individual blocks. Returned by blocks.getById(), blocks.getBlockByIndex(), and blocks.insert().

caretApi.title: Caret API
caretApi.description: Control cursor position and selection within the editor.

eventsApi.title: Events API
eventsApi.description: Subscribe to and manage editor lifecycle events.

historyApi.title: History API
historyApi.description: Control undo/redo functionality for editor operations.

selectionApi.title: Selection API
selectionApi.description: Work with text selection within the editor.

stylesApi.title: Styles API
stylesApi.description: Access CSS class names for styling custom tools and UI elements.

toolbarApi.title: Toolbar API
toolbarApi.description: Control the block toolbar and its state.

inlineToolbarApi.title: InlineToolbar API
inlineToolbarApi.description: Control the inline formatting toolbar (bold, italic, etc.).

sanitizerApi.title: Sanitizer API
sanitizerApi.description: Clean and sanitize HTML content to prevent XSS attacks.

tooltipApi.title: Tooltip API
tooltipApi.description: Display tooltip hints on UI elements.

readOnlyApi.title: ReadOnly API
readOnlyApi.description: Control the read-only state of the editor.

i18nApi.title: I18n API
i18nApi.description: Internationalization support for translating UI strings.

uiApi.title: UI API
uiApi.description: Access to Blok UI elements and state.

listenersApi.title: Listeners API
listenersApi.description: Manage custom DOM event listeners with automatic cleanup.

outputData.title: OutputData
outputData.description: The data structure returned by the save() method.

blockData.title: BlockData
blockData.description: The structure of each block in the blocks array.

hero.title: Build beautiful
hero.description: A production-ready, extensible rich text editor that brings Notion-like block-based editing to your app — customizable, themeable, and battle-tested.
hero.mascotAlt: Blok mascot - a friendly orange character with pink yarn

features.description: Everything you need to create powerful editing experiences in your applications.

cleanJson.title: Clean JSON Output
cleanJson.description: Content is structured as typed JSON blocks, not raw HTML. Parse, store, and render anywhere.
cleanJson.learnMore: Learn more about Clean JSON Output

details.summary: Blok outputs clean, typed JSON data that's easy to store, transform, and render in any environment.
details.benefit1: Store content in any database without HTML parsing
details.benefit2: Render on server, client, or native apps
details.benefit3: Full TypeScript types for all block data

slashCommands.title: Toolbox & Slash Commands
slashCommands.description: Press / or click + to open the block menu. Insert paragraphs, headers, lists, and custom blocks instantly.
slashCommands.learnMore: Learn more about Toolbox & Slash Commands

details.summary: A powerful command palette for quick block insertion. Users can type "/" to search and insert any block type.
details.benefit2: Searchable block menu with fuzzy matching
details.benefit3: Custom shortcuts for frequently used blocks

inlineToolbar.title: Inline Toolbar
inlineToolbar.description: Select text to format with bold, italic, and links. Extensible with custom inline tools.

details.summary: A floating toolbar appears when text is selected, providing quick access to inline formatting options.
details.benefit2: Built-in bold, italic, and link tools
details.benefit4: Smart positioning that stays in viewport

dragDrop.title: Drag & Drop
dragDrop.description: Rearrange blocks with drag handles. Pointer-based system for desktop devices.

details.summary: Intuitive drag and drop reordering using a pointer-based system. Currently available on desktop devices.

customBlocks.title: Custom Block Tools
customBlocks.description: Extend the editor with custom blocks. Built-in paragraph, header, list tools included.
customBlocks.learnMore: Learn more about Custom Block Tools

details.summary: Create any block type you need. From simple text blocks to complex interactive components.
details.benefit1: Simple API for creating custom blocks
details.benefit2: Built-in paragraph, header, and list tools

readOnly.title: Read-Only Mode
readOnly.description: Toggle read-only mode at runtime. Perfect for view-only contexts or permission-based editing.

details.summary: Switch between edit and read-only modes at runtime without reinitializing the editor.

undoRedo.title: Undo & Redo
undoRedo.description: CRDT-based history with ⌘Z / ⌘⇧Z. Conflict-free state tracking for reliable undo/redo.

details.summary: CRDT-powered undo/redo ensures conflict-free history tracking. Keyboard shortcuts and API access included.

languages.title: 68 Languages
languages.description: Lazy-loaded i18n with RTL support. Only English bundled by default ~3KB.

details.summary: Full internationalization with 68 languages, RTL support, and lazy loading for optimal bundle size.

smartPaste.title: Smart Paste
smartPaste.description: Paste from Google Docs or plain text. HTML is sanitized and converted to blocks.

details.summary: Paste content from Google Docs and watch it transform into clean, structured blocks automatically.
details.benefit1: Works with Google Docs and plain text
details.benefit4: Extensible paste handlers for custom tools

quickStart.title: Up and running in minutes
quickStart.description: Three simple steps to integrate Blok into your project

apiPreview.description: Access every aspect of the editor through our comprehensive API surface.

migrationCard.title: Migrate from EditorJS
migrationCard.description: Already using EditorJS? Our automated codemod handles most of the transition — just run it, review the changes, and ship.

footer.tagline: A friendly block-based rich text editor for modern applications.

search.noResultsDescription: Try different keywords or browse the documentation sections
search.emptyDescription: Find API methods, configuration options, tools, and more

demo.title: Try the
demo.subtitle: A fully interactive editor running right here in your browser. Type
demo.subtitleRest: for commands, drag blocks to reorder, or select text to format.
demo.hintInstantFeedbackDesc: Everything happens locally in your browser. No server roundtrips, just pure performance.
demo.hintCleanJsonDesc: Content is stored as structured JSON blocks. Parse, validate, and store anywhere.
demo.hintBlockArchDesc: Create custom block types with simple JavaScript classes. Fully extensible.
demo.outputInitialMessage: Click "Get JSON" to see the output

editorWrapper.buildHint: Make sure the Blok editor is built with

demo.welcomeParagraph: This is a live demo of the Blok editor. Try typing / to see available commands, or select text to format it.
demo.welcomeListItem3: Drag and drop to reorder blocks

recipes.heroDescription: Practical tips, patterns, and code snippets to help you get the most out of Blok. From basic setup to advanced customization.
recipes.ctaDescription: Your patterns and techniques could help thousands of developers build better editors.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Reference
shortcuts.subtitle: Master Blok with these essential shortcuts
shortcuts.moreShortcuts: More shortcuts available in the toolbox menu

recipes.autosaveDescription: Automatically save content as users type, with debouncing to prevent excessive server requests.
recipes.autosaveTip: A 1-second debounce is usually a good balance between responsiveness and server load.
recipes.eventsDescription: Listen to editor events to integrate with your application's state management or analytics.
recipes.customToolDescription: Build your own block type to extend Blok's functionality. This example creates a simple alert/callout block.
recipes.customToolTip: Keep tools focused on a single purpose. For complex needs, compose multiple simple tools.
recipes.stylingDescription: Customize Blok's appearance using CSS and data attributes. No need to fight with specificity.
recipes.readOnlyDescription: Display saved content without editing capabilities, or toggle between edit and preview modes.
recipes.readOnlyTip: Read-only mode is perfect for previewing content before publishing or displaying user-generated content.
recipes.localizationDescription: Configure Blok for multiple languages with optional preloading for offline support or instant initialization.
recipes.localizationTip: Most apps can use on-demand loading with buildRegistry() directly—the ~50-100ms delay is usually imperceptible.

migration.heroDescription: Migrate your project in minutes using our automated codemod.
migration.step1Description: One command handles imports, selectors, types, and configuration automatically.
migration.step2Description: The codemod handles all the breaking changes automatically.
migration.step3Description: Reference for manually updating your CSS selectors.
migration.ctaDescription: Start with the automated codemod and complete your migration in minutes.
migration.codemodApplyLabel: Apply the changes to your files
migration.codemodVerboseDescription: Show detailed output for each file processed

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.description: Track every improvement, fix, and feature as Blok evolves

integrations.heroDescription1: The official React adapter ships as
integrations.heroDescription4: component that manage the full editor lifecycle — including StrictMode, SSR-safe rendering, and clean teardown.
integrations.installNote: React 18+ is a peer dependency — it is not bundled with Blok. If you already have React in your project, no extra install is needed.

setup.title: Installation
setup.description: Install Blok and its React adapter. React 18 or higher is required as a peer dependency.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.description: Import useBlok and BlokContent from @jackuait/blok/react. The hook manages the editor lifecycle; the component mounts the editor DOM into your layout.

useBlok.title: useBlok
useBlok.description: A React hook that creates and manages a Blok editor instance. Returns the editor once it is ready, or null during initialization.

blokContent.title: BlokContent
blokContent.description: A component that mounts the editor's DOM tree into your layout. Renders an empty div when editor is null, maintaining layout stability while the editor initializes.

readOnly.title: Read-Only Mode
readOnly.description: Toggle read-only mode reactively by passing readOnly to useBlok. The editor updates via editor.readOnly.set() without recreation.

switchingDocs.title: Switching Documents
switchingDocs.description: Pass a deps array as the second argument to useBlok to recreate the editor when your document changes. The old instance is destroyed cleanly before the new one is created.

fullExample.title: Complete Example
fullExample.description: A full blog editor component using useBlok and BlokContent together.
